title: Indie iOS app launch task model

tasks.action: Write a one-sentence value proposition naming the audience, problem, and advantage over the current alternative.
tasks.rationale: This sentence anchors screenshots, description, website, pitches, and launch copy.
tasks.done_when: One specific sentence is approved and avoids vague category language.

tasks.action: Identify direct competitors and indirect alternatives, including manual workflows and doing nothing.
tasks.rationale: Positioning must compete with the user's real alternatives, not only similar apps.
tasks.done_when: A comparison lists direct apps, indirect substitutes, strengths, weaknesses, price, and positioning.

tasks.action: Choose three core App Store search keywords and inspect the live results on a real device.
tasks.rationale: Early search research informs positioning and later provides an ASO baseline.
tasks.done_when: Three terms are documented with search intent, current leaders, and why the app can compete.

tasks.action: Select the business model and an initial price: paid, freemium, or subscription.
tasks.rationale: A testable pricing hypothesis is more useful than leaving monetization undecided until submission.
tasks.done_when: The model, price points, trial or free tier, and reasoned hypothesis are recorded.

tasks.action: Create the app record in App Store Connect.
tasks.rationale: Creating the record secures the intended listing name and unlocks submission setup.
tasks.done_when: The correct bundle ID, SKU, primary language, and app record are visible in App Store Connect.

tasks.action: Buy the domain and publish a minimal landing page with name, value proposition, screenshot, and email signup.
tasks.rationale: A simple public destination can collect interest before the store page is available.
tasks.done_when: The HTTPS page works on mobile, signup is tested, and analytics or signup delivery can be checked.

tasks.action: Start building in public by discussing the user's problem before promoting the app.
tasks.rationale: Problem-focused posts find the first people who care and generate language for later messaging.
tasks.done_when: The first useful problem-focused post is published and responses are captured.

tasks.action: Draft App Store metadata as one system: name, subtitle, and keyword field without wasteful repetition.
tasks.rationale: The visible name and subtitle sell while the hidden keywords support discovery.
tasks.done_when: Metadata meets current Apple limits, reflects positioning, and avoids prohibited or duplicate terms.

tasks.action: Create required App Store screenshots, prioritizing the first three frames and every supported device family.
tasks.rationale: Screenshots are both a submission requirement and a primary conversion surface.
tasks.done_when: Current required sizes upload successfully and the first three frames communicate outcomes clearly.

tasks.action: Write the App Store description as sales copy: outcome first, features second.
tasks.rationale: Outcome-led copy tells prospective users why the app matters before listing implementation details.
tasks.done_when: The description is specific, scannable, accurate, localized as needed, and within Apple's current limit.

tasks.action: Submit an App Store featuring nomination for the launch.
tasks.rationale: Apple asks developers to share noteworthy launches in advance and recommends at least three weeks of lead time.
tasks.done_when: A submitted nomination includes the launch date, differentiated story, relevant markets, and supporting links.

tasks.action: Complete App Store Connect blockers: app privacy, age rating, support URL, review contact, notes, and demo access when needed.
tasks.rationale: Missing compliance or reviewer information causes preventable submission delays.
tasks.done_when: Every required field is complete, URLs work, reviewer steps are reproducible, and demo credentials do not expire.

tasks.action: Start external TestFlight beta testing and provide focused What to Test guidance.
tasks.rationale: The first external build requires review and testers need time to reveal real defects.
tasks.done_when: The first external group can install an approved build and feedback has a defined collection path.

tasks.action: Submit an approvable build for App Review, configure manual release, and prepare or publish the pre-order when eligible.
tasks.rationale: Early review creates margin and an approved pre-order converts prelaunch interest into release-day installs.
tasks.done_when: The intended build is submitted, release settings are verified, and the pre-order page is live or its blocker is documented.

tasks.action: Share the pre-order link across the website, email list, profiles, and relevant build-in-public posts.
tasks.rationale: A direct pre-order action turns diffuse interest into an automatic launch-day download.
tasks.done_when: Every owned channel uses the correct tracked URL and the page resolves in target storefronts.

tasks.action: Collect at least three permissioned pieces of social proof from beta use or early feedback.
tasks.rationale: Credible evidence supports launch posts, press materials, and product-page messaging.
tasks.done_when: Three attributable, permissioned quotes, usage examples, or screenshots are stored with context.

tasks.action: Assemble a press kit with fact sheet, full-resolution screenshots, app icon, contact details, links, and concise story angles.
tasks.rationale: A complete, easy-to-download package reduces work for journalists and creators.
tasks.done_when: One public link exposes current assets and facts without requiring access permission.

tasks.action: Pitch selected journalists and creators with a short personal note, access, press kit, and clear release date.
tasks.rationale: Two weeks gives recipients time to evaluate and publish without making the launch easy to forget.
tasks.done_when: A focused contact list is pitched, follow-ups are scheduled, and responses are tracked.

tasks.action: Enable App Store Connect notifications for review and status changes.
tasks.rationale: Fast awareness prevents a rejection, metadata question, or rating from sitting unnoticed.
tasks.done_when: The correct team members receive and test the relevant notification channels.

tasks.action: Write every launch-day publication and reusable reply in advance.
tasks.rationale: Prepared copy protects quality when launch-day attention is fragmented.
tasks.done_when: Website copy, email, social posts, launch article, FAQs, and expected replies are approved and scheduled or staged.

tasks.action: Submit the final reviewed release candidate or another corrected version with a full week of margin.
tasks.rationale: A seven-day buffer absorbs a slow review or a correctable rejection.
tasks.done_when: The intended release candidate is submitted, smoke-tested, and its review status is monitored.

tasks.action: Prepare the Product Hunt page when the app and audience fit that channel.
tasks.rationale: Gallery assets, maker story, and first comment are too important to improvise on launch day.
tasks.done_when: The page, gallery, first comment, maker story, links, and response owner are ready, or the channel is marked not applicable.
tasks.applies_when: Product Hunt is a relevant acquisition channel.

tasks.action: Freeze noncritical changes and rest before launch.
tasks.rationale: Last-minute feature work creates avoidable risk before a customer-service-heavy day.
tasks.done_when: Only release blockers may change, the approved build is identified, and launch owners know the next day's runbook.

tasks.action: Release the approved app or verify that the pre-order transitions to available.
tasks.rationale: Release execution should use the already-approved build and settings.
tasks.done_when: The intended version is downloadable in target storefronts and a purchase or download smoke test passes.

tasks.action: Replace the website signup or pre-order CTA with the live App Store download CTA.
tasks.rationale: Owned traffic must point to the actual download action as soon as the app is available.
tasks.done_when: The production website uses the live store link and tracking works.

tasks.action: Publish the prepared launch post and email the list.
tasks.rationale: Prepared owned-channel messages create the first coordinated wave of awareness.
tasks.done_when: All selected owned channels are live with correct links and no stale pre-order language.

tasks.action: Launch on Product Hunt early in the US day when that channel is part of the plan.
tasks.rationale: A prepared launch page benefits from a full day of audience activity and responses.
tasks.done_when: The page is live, links work, and a maker is available to respond, or the channel is marked not applicable.
tasks.applies_when: Product Hunt is a relevant acquisition channel.

tasks.action: Spend the rest of launch day replying to comments, questions, mentions, and support requests.
tasks.rationale: Responsive conversation is the day's primary marketing and support work.
tasks.done_when: Incoming conversations are acknowledged, bugs are logged, and urgent issues have owners.

tasks.action: Centralize every feedback source in one log.
tasks.rationale: Patterns become visible only when reviews, mail, social replies, crashes, and beta notes are combined.
tasks.done_when: Each item has source, date, severity, theme, owner, status, and a link to evidence.

tasks.action: Submit the app to relevant app directories.
tasks.rationale: Directory discovery can create a useful long tail beyond the launch-day spike.
tasks.done_when: A qualified list is submitted with tracked links and each submission status is recorded.

tasks.action: Fix the highest-severity post-launch defect and request expedited review only for a genuine critical case.
tasks.rationale: Fast, focused repair protects early trust; expedited review is an exceptional path, not normal scheduling.
tasks.done_when: The defect is reproduced, fixed, tested, release-noted, and submitted through the appropriate review path.

tasks.action: Reply respectfully and specifically to App Store reviews, including critical reviews.
tasks.rationale: Future customers read developer responses as evidence of support quality.
tasks.done_when: New reviews have a response or an explicit priority reason, and no reply exposes private information.

tasks.action: Ship version 1.0.1 only when a tested fix or useful improvement earns the update.
tasks.rationale: A meaningful first-week update shows that the app is maintained and feedback matters.
tasks.done_when: The update passes regression testing, App Review, release monitoring, and communicates concrete changes.

tasks.action: Enable the system review prompt at a natural moment of user success after the first update settles.
tasks.rationale: Satisfied, experienced users can provide more meaningful ratings than users interrupted at first launch.
tasks.done_when: The prompt uses Apple's API, avoids onboarding and interruption, and has version and engagement safeguards.

tasks.action: Record ranking baselines for the three core keywords.
tasks.rationale: A dated baseline makes later ASO changes measurable.
tasks.done_when: Rank, storefront, device platform, search-result competitors, and measurement date are stored for each term.

tasks.action: Plan the next meaningful update and treat it as a smaller relaunch.
tasks.rationale: A two-to-four-week update cadence creates repeated opportunities for visibility and retention.
tasks.done_when: The next release has a focused scope, owner, target date, test plan, and communication plan.

tasks.action: Revisit the product page using real keyword, conversion, review, and retention data.
tasks.rationale: ASO decisions become better grounded after the app has actual traffic and outcomes.
tasks.done_when: Proposed metadata or creative changes cite measured evidence and define the next comparison window.

tasks.action: Decide whether Apple Ads is economically viable before spending.
tasks.rationale: Product-page conversion, paywall conversion, net lifetime value, and allowable acquisition cost determine whether paid growth can work.
tasks.done_when: The funnel math and maximum acquisition cost are documented; a small test has guardrails or ads are deferred while the funnel is fixed.

tasks.action: Publish a build-in-public launch retrospective with real numbers and mistakes.
tasks.rationale: Transparent lessons can create another wave of useful attention and force honest learning.
tasks.done_when: The retrospective includes outcomes, misses, decisions, user feedback, and specific changes for the next launch.

tasks.action: Review Apple Ads results and keyword ranks, stop ineffective work, and roll lessons into the next update.
tasks.rationale: The final launch task converts campaign learning into the routine operation of the app.
tasks.done_when: Keep, change, and stop decisions are backed by data and assigned to the next release plan.
